title: Cracian thumper
content:

{{Quote|The Thumper got its name from the sound it ''should'' make when it runs. However, the remarkably agile creatures have such a light touch that they hardly make any sound at all.|[[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]]|<ref name="The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook">''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]''</ref>}}
'''Cracian thumpers''' were bipedal herbivores used as pack and riding animals. Originally from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Craci]] in the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]], they possessed clawed [[Limb/Legends|forelimbs]] and hindlimbs, and a powerful tail. Their large, padded feet allowed them to move very quietly, earning them their ironic name—the "thumper"—which implied it should have thumped as it ran. They were loyal creatures, and unusually bright for a beast of burden; they were capable of being trained to perform a number of different tasks, to recognize patterns, and to follow simple commands. Though normally inoffensive, their claws and tail made them deadly opponents when they were threatened or trained to fight.

The usefulness of Cracian thumpers as riding and pack animals saw them heavily exported, making them the most widely employed land mount in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. They were frequently used in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], where they were an important part of its live mount [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] program, and were also heavily utilized by [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|Alliance SpecForce]] personnel. Cracian thumper racing was also a popular sport in urban areas such as the [[Tapani sector/Legends|Tapani sector]].

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|Sharp foreclaws and a whipping tail provide these creatures with remarkable offensive capabilities.|Voren Na'al|<ref name="The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook" />}}
Thumpers were agile, bipedal creatures, with a powerful whipping tail, and with hindlimbs and shorter forelimbs that were both sharply clawed.<ref name="Rebel Alliance Sourcebook" /> They were herbivorous and had hard, flat teeth for eating vegetable matter.<ref name="The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook" /> Cracian thumpers in cold climates possessed long, thick fur; in warmer locales, this coat could be shed.<ref name="Rebellion Era Campaign Guide">''[[Rebellion Era Campaign Guide]]''</ref> Their feet were large<ref name="Rebellion Era Campaign Guide" /> and padded,<ref name="Rules of Engagement: The Rebel SpecForce Handbook">''[[Rules of Engagement: The Rebel SpecForce Handbook]]''</ref> features which gave the creatures their ironic name; Cracian thumpers should have thumped as they ran, but they made nearly no sound at all.<ref name="Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded">''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> A fully-loaded thumper could carry a load of one hundred kilograms, and could travel at thirty-five kilometers per hour.<ref name="Rules of Engagement: The Rebel SpecForce Handbook" /> Cracian thumpers were thought to be genetic relatives of the [[rockhopper]]s of [[Roon/Legends|Roon]] and [[Biitu]], which may have evolved from imported thumper populations.<ref name="TUR ">''[[The Unknown Regions]]''</ref>

==Behavior==
{{Quote|They are extremely loyal and obedient animals. They can be taught to follow verbal or touch commands, and can even distinguish between friends and enemies if their masters train them.|Voren Na'al|<ref name="The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook" />}}
Cracian thumpers were very faithful, amenable, and largely non-aggressive creatures.<ref name="The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook" /> They were quite smart for beasts of burden, with intelligence more typical of domestic pets.<ref name="Rebel Alliance Sourcebook" /> Versatile enough to be trained for a wide variety of tasks, thumpers were particularly adept at rudimentary pattern recognition.<ref name="Rebellion Era Campaign Guide" /> They could learn to follow verbal and touch commands, to distinguish between friends and enemies when properly trained by their masters, a task made easier when "enemies" had such easily identifiable characteristics as the hard white shell of an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]]. Some could even be taught to attack on command.<ref name="Rebel Alliance Sourcebook" />

When roused to fight, either when threatened or when so trained, thumpers were formidable opponents.<ref name="Rebel Alliance Sourcebook" /><ref name="The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook" /> A thumper would attack with its sharp foreclaws and by whipping its tail; the latter was capable of beheading a stormtrooper with ease. They were also capable of lashing out backwards with the sharp claws on their hindlimbs while at a full run.<ref name="Rebel Alliance Sourcebook" />

==History==
{{Quote|When the Cracians decided to market them to the galaxy, Thumpers became indispensable for use as both riding and pack animals.|Voren Na'al|<ref name="The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook" />}}
Cracian thumpers originated on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Craci]]<ref name="Edge of the Empire" /> and could be found on all five worlds of the [[Craci system/Legends|Craci system]],<ref name="Rebel Alliance Sourcebook" /> an agricultural and industrial hub that was one of the busiest ports and trading centers in the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]].<ref name="The Essential Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Long used by the native [[Cracian]]s as beasts of burden, thumpers proved to be indispensable mounts and pack beasts when marketed to [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] at large.<ref name="The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook" /> By the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], there were millions of thumpers on [[Human/Legends|Human]]-populated worlds,<ref name="Rebel Alliance Sourcebook" /> making them one of the most common riding beasts in the civilized galaxy<ref name="Rules of Engagement: The Rebel SpecForce Handbook" /> and the most widely used riding beast on land.<ref name="Rebellion Era Campaign Guide" /> The inhabitants of the planet [[Rodia/Legends|Rodia]] also used imported Cracian thumpers as live mounts in order to traverse the planet's jungles.<ref name="SotE PG">''[[Shadows of the Empire Planets Guide]]''</ref>

[[File:Cracian_thumper1.jpg|thumb|left||250px|A [[Unidentified Jomark messenger|colonist]] on Cracian thumperback brings a message to Joruus C'baoth's High Castle.]]
When the first colonists of [[Jomark/Legends|Jomark]] left for their new world a few thousand years prior to the Galactic Civil War, they brought several Cracian thumpers with them. This small seed population soon bloomed, and by the time of the [[Thrawn campaign]], herds of wild thumpers plied the forests of the main continent and a few of the larger islands of the planet, in addition to the many domestic thumpers still used by the colonists for a number of tasks.<ref name="The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook" /> A [[Unidentified Jomark messenger|colonist]] sent to bring a message to the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Joruus C'baoth]] in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]] rode a thumper over the steep roadway to C'baoth's [[High Castle]].<ref name="Dark Force Rising">''[[Dark Force Rising]]''</ref>

Cracian thumper racing was a very popular sport in urban areas,<ref name="Rebellion Era Campaign Guide" /> especially within the [[Tapani sector/Legends|Tapani sector]].<ref name="Tapani Sector Instant Adventures">''[[Tapani Sector Instant Adventures]]''</ref> Racing thumpers generally had their coats shaved off to reduce their weight and provide a sleeker appearance.<ref name="Rebellion Era Campaign Guide" /> The [[Tapani Arts and Sports Complex]] on [[Vycinyth]] maintained large stadiums and arenas suitable for a number of sports, including thumper races.<ref name="Lords of the Expanse">''[[Lords of the Expanse]]''</ref> The [[Heatherdowns Hotel and Track]] on [[Tallaan]] maintained a five-kilometer oval track for Cracian thumper racing, ringed by thousands of seats and bleachers. It also maintained gardens and training facilities in the center of the track.<ref name="Tapani Sector Instant Adventures" />

During the era of the Galactic Empire, [[Rothana Heavy Engineering/Legends|Rothana Heavy Engineering]] included Cracian thumpers in their catalog of mounted creatures available to Imperial base commanders. Around [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], Cracian thumpers were listed in the ''[[Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]'', an Imperial commander manual, at 2,000 credits each, with specialty breeds ranging from 5,000 to 7,000 credits. Rothana advertised thumpers as being ideal for [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] an enemy line, as they remained calm under fire, could transport heavy loads, and could run steady paces for hours while also remaining quiet.<ref name="Handbook" />

[[File:Cracian-Thumper.jpg|thumb|right||200px|A Rebel scout riding a Cracian thumper.]]
The Cracian thumper was used for several functions within the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], which turned to the thumper and similar beasts as replacements for hardware made unavailable by Imperial restriction.<ref name="Rebellion Era Campaign Guide" /> It was one of the primary live mounts used by Rebel scouts; live mounts like the thumper were more difficult for Imperial [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] to detect than vehicles like [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bikes]], and, if detected, were likely to be dismissed as a wild animal. The ubiquitous presence of thumpers on planets across the galaxy meant an Imperial sensor operator would not find the presence of the beast out of place on nearly any world on which he was stationed; if lazy, there was less than a fifty percent chance he would send a patrol to investigate.<ref name="Rebel Alliance Sourcebook" /> Among the outposts where the Alliance used thumpers were the planets [[Fangol]], [[Nishr]], and [[Dankayo/Legends|Dankayo]].<ref name="Handbook" /> The success the Rebel Alliance had with the Cracian thumper and other pack animals led them to quickly domesticate several of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth's]] [[Tauntaun/Legends|tauntauns]] as mounts when establishing [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]].<ref name="Galactic Gazetteer: Hoth and the Greater Javin">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20040325web|text=Galactic Gazetteer: Hoth and the Greater Javin|int=Galactic Gazetteer: Hoth and the Greater Javin}}</ref> Alliance [[General/Legends|General]] [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]], commander of Echo Base, would later note that thumpers were significantly more agreeable than tauntauns in margin notes he wrote in a copy of ''Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide'' that had fallen into the hands of [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]].<ref name="Handbook" />

Thumpers were also popular with [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|Alliance Special Forces]]. Their frequency of appearance meant [[Wilderness Fighters]] and [[Pathfinders/Legends|Pathfinders]] could use them on thousands of planets without drawing attention, while [[Infiltrators/Legends|Infiltrators]] enjoyed the incredibly stealthy nature of the creatures.<ref name="Rules of Engagement: The Rebel SpecForce Handbook" /> [[General/Legends|General]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]], the [[Supreme Commander (Alliance)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] of SpecForce, was experienced in riding the animals,<ref name="Dark Empire Sourcebook">''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]''</ref> as was the [[Jin]] [[Foster Agent]] [[Dutra Zeneta]], legendary veteran of SpecForce's [[Scandium Team]].<ref name="CRO">{{JournalCite|10|Cracken's Rebel Operatives (article)}}</ref>

[[Barosa Warren]], the [[Morellian]] founder of the galaxy-renowned [[Galactic Outdoor Survival School/Legends|Galactic Outdoor Survival School]], was familiar with Cracian thumpers.<ref name="AIR">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref> The [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] tour guide [[Meebo]] kept a dozen Cracian thumpers at [[Meebo's|his facility]] on [[Dalicron-4]] and used the beasts for his hunting trips. When Rebel agents sent by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] came to Dalicron-4 approximately four months after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], [[Jhara]], the granddaughter of [[Gel (Jedi)|the man]] who sent for Skywalker, acquired several thumpers from Meebo as transportation from the village of [[Dalia (township)|Dalia]] to her family farm. Upon finding her grandfather dead, Jhara and the Rebels set out on these same mounts to find [[Caeleb]], a child with exceptional [[The Force/Legends|Force]] potential and the objective of the Rebels' mission.<ref name="Child of Light">"[[Child of Light]]"</ref> The phrase "like a mad [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] on Cracian thumperback," was a metaphor for someone coming to another person's rescue in a reckless, impulsive manner.<ref name="Side Trip">"[[Side Trip]]"</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Tauntaun_concept.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Early tauntaun concept art]]
The Cracian thumpers were first described in the ''[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook]]'' published by [[West End Games]] in [[1990]], with their visual representation drawn from unused concept art for the tauntauns seen in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']].<ref name="Alien species concepts that became part of the SW universe">{{Blog|teekay-421|69|Alien species concepts that became part of the SW universe}}</ref> They were repeated in a number of other [[roleplaying game]] sourcebooks, but made their first appearance in [[Timothy Zahn]]'s novel ''[[Dark Force Rising]]'', published in [[1992]]. Capitalization of the creature's name has varied between "Cracian thumper,"<ref name="Rebel Alliance Sourcebook" /><ref name="Lords of the Expanse" /><ref name="Galactic Gazetteer: Hoth and the Greater Javin" /><ref name="Child of Light" /> "Cracian Thumper,"<ref name="The Essential Atlas" /><ref name="Child of Light" /><ref name="Dark Force Rising Sourcebook">''[[Dark Force Rising Sourcebook]]''</ref> and "cracian thumper"<ref name="Rebellion Era Campaign Guide" /> across sources.

Thumpers share a very similar outward physiology to another riding beast, the [[rockhopper]] of [[Biitu]] and [[Roon/Legends|Roon]],<ref>Compare [[:File:Rockhopper-TGH1.jpg|this image]]. Commentators who have noted the similarity include [[Curtis Saxton]], who mentions it in the [http://www.theforce.net/swtc/highlights/heep.html#thumper Highlights: The Great Heep] page at his ''[[Curtis Saxton|Star Wars Technical Commentaries'']].</ref> a similarity that was acknowledged as likely genetic relation in a section on rockhoppers in [[Wizards of the Coast]]'s [[2010]] publication ''[[The Unknown Regions]]''.
